text: Exercise therapy has the highest level of evidence for treating Achilles tendinopathy and should be the initial treatment for at least 3 months before considering other options.
Recovery from Achilles tendinopathy can take up to a year.
There is a high propensity for Achilles tendinopathy recurrence, especially during the return-to-sport phase.
Return-to-sport planning for Achilles tendinopathy must account for the extent of tendon injury, athlete age and sex, magnitude of pain/symptoms, extent of impairments, and demands of the sport.
Achilles tendinopathy is a common overuse injury in athletes involved in running and jumping activities.
